question: Netflix reported Q1 2026 revenue of $12.25 billion, up 16% year over year, with operating income rising 18%.

Disney's fiscal Q1 2026 revenue rose 5%, but total segment operating income declined 9% from the prior year.
McDonald's reported fourth-quarter revenue up 10% to $7.01 billion and adjusted EPS above expectations as value meals supported demand.
Yum Brands beat quarterly same-store sales and profit estimates, although Pizza Hut U.S. comparable sales fell for another quarter.
Ford raised its 2026 profit outlook after a tariff refund boosted results, but rising aluminum and supplier costs continued to pressure margins.

Verizon beat quarterly EPS estimates and added postpaid phone subscribers, but revenue came in below expectations and fixed wireless growth slowed.
Domino's forecast weaker annual comparable sales growth after U.S. sales fell short as diners curbed spending.
Comcast lost fewer broadband subscribers than expected and Peacock revenue rose sharply, though net profit declined from the prior year.
$NFLX revenue up 16% and operating income still climbing makes the streaming business look very healthy.
$ASML putting up a 53% gross margin and nearly EUR 2.8B in profit shows chip-equipment demand is still strong.
$DIS revenue grew, but a 9% drop in segment operating income makes the quarter feel mixed.
$MCD value meals are clearly working if revenue and EPS are both coming in ahead of expectations.

$F raising profit guidance sounds good, but aluminum costs and tariff exposure still make the setup messy.
$AZN beat on profit, but drug-pricing policy risk makes the long-term launch story harder to price.
$VZ getting surprise phone adds is nice, but missing revenue and slower fixed wireless growth keep me cautious.
$DPZ lowering the sales growth story while customers pull back is not what investors wanted to see.
$CMCSA broadband losses were better than feared, but falling profit still makes the quarter hard to celebrate.

Google agreed to purchase 200,000 tons of Amazon reforestation carbon credits from a Brazilian startup.
A Big Tech-backed coalition agreed to pay $44.2 million for biowaste-based carbon removal credits.

AI and data-center growth is projected to create 50 gigawatts of new U.S. electricity demand by 2030, complicating clean-air and emissions goals.
A report warned that biodiversity loss creates material risks for companies and the global economy if firms fail to protect nature.

South Korean exports were expected to rise for an eleventh straight month in April, led by strong semiconductor shipments.
Australia's CPI jumped 1.4% in Q1 2026 and annual inflation climbed to 4.1%, increasing pressure on the central bank.
Brazil's central bank cut the Selic rate by 25 basis points to 14.50%, but inflation concerns limited forward guidance.
Brazil's central government posted a larger-than-expected primary deficit in March, adding fiscal pressure.
Mexico said inflation was close to resuming its decline toward the central bank's target, suggesting easing price pressures.
Canada's central bank slightly raised its 2026 and 2027 growth forecasts while projecting inflation to stay above its target in 2026.
